author: m.kalinin
content: I think that case was with almost perfect synchrony when nodes are able to process payload envelops in time and thus agree on the view. Although, if we consider a case similar to Holesky, nodes would likely disagree on the head of the beacon chain rather than the payload status of the previous slot.

author: potuz
content: The situation in which nodes disagree on the payload status on top of the head root happens if we do increase the gas limit enough so as to payloads not being executable in the alloted time. When the chain is split across shufflings there won't even be ptc consensus on either timely nor untimely, so proposers are likely able to reorg payloads at will. This is due to current limitations on PTC members to track all shufflings to see in which branch they need to attest. Given that we have a different situation with PTC than attesters, I think we should solve this (at least on Prysm we do ) and do send attestations on the different shufflings that we are members of.
